oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Using stifle joint evaluations to assess the occurrence of sepsis and osteochondrosis lesions in feedyard cattle
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Geesling, Kayley
White, Brad
Larson, Robert
Feitoza, Luis
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Opening a stifle joint is an underutilized examination in field necropsy of feedyard cattle, and limited research has been con­ducted to characterize the prevalence of abnormal findings. Stifle examination at necropsy may provide valuable information regarding economic- and welfare-associated diseases. The study objective was to determine the frequency of sepsis and osteo­chondrosis (OC) lesions in stifles of necropsied feedyard cattle.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 236
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
